topics.title: Steve Grillo's Radio Show and Movie Reviews
topics.summary: The studio discusses the career of former intern Steve Grillo, his transition from an electrician to a movie reviewer on radio, and his controversial review of a film about 9/11.

topics.title: Drunk Driving and Hollywood Career Discussion
topics.summary: The studio features a conversation regarding the consequences of driving under the influence, the experiences of actress Jodie Foster on film sets, and the nuances of celebrity life.

topics.title: Movie Reviews and Karaoke Performances
topics.summary: The studio features a humorous review of a World Trade Center film using a "potato" rating system followed by a discussion regarding a prank call and a karaoke segment featuring Artie Lange.

topics.title: Satellite Radio Success and Media Criticism
topics.summary: The studio discusses the growth of satellite radio listenership, the impact of YouTube on media consumption, and a specific news article that inaccurately reported on the show's transition to satellite.

topics.title: Early Academic Experiences and SAT Preparation
topics.summary: The studio explores personal anecdotes regarding high school experiences, including a stint in a prestigious club, the use of test-prep courses for the SATs, and the transition to college life.

topics.title: Satellite Radio Growth and Media Coverage
topics.summary: The show features a discussion regarding a news article's portrayal of satellite radio success, specifically addressing listener numbers, market leadership, and the impact of high-profile personalities on retail sales.

topics.title: Parents Television Council Leadership Change
topics.summary: The show discusses the resignation of Elle Brent-Bozell as president of the Parents Television Council and the potential reasons behind her departure.

topics.title: Karaoke Contest and Voicemail Tapes
topics.summary: The studio reviews a series of karaoke performances from the previous day, discusses technical issues with audio mixing, and plays several drunk voicemail messages left by a regular caller.

topics.title: Waffle House Calls and Guest Interviews
topics.summary: The show features a series of segments including a prank call to a Waffle House, a discussion regarding a comedy film, an analysis of Paris Hilton's DUI statement, and an interview with comedian Doug Stanhope and his girlfriend.

topics.title: Relationship with a Mental Patient
topics.summary: The studio explores the experiences of a comedian dating a woman with schizoaffective bipolar disorder, discussing her past hospitalizations, erratic behaviors during manic episodes, and their shared life together.

topics.title: Comedy Tour Anecdotes and Drug Use
topics.summary: The studio explores a comedian's experiences with audience reactions, the impact of substance use on stage performance, and his humorous political aspirations.

topics.title: Comedy Performance and Sexual Dynamics
topics.summary: The studio features a discussion regarding the use of substances during comedy sets, personal sexual experiences, and a demonstration involving a vibrator.

topics.title: A Massage Gone Wrong
topics.summary: The studio features a guest recounting an incident where she was subjected to unwanted sexual advances during a massage from an elderly man.

topics.title: Media Violence and Vanity Fair Power List
topics.summary: The studio reviews a news segment regarding a reporter's physical assault by a suspect and discusses the criteria for inclusion on the Vanity Fair power list.

topics.title: Staff Tensions and News Segment Highlights
topics.summary: The studio experiences internal friction regarding staff roles and personal space before transitioning into news segments covering Hollywood gossip, Warren Buffett's family disputes, and the death of Steve Irwin.

topics.title: Current Events and Sports News
topics.summary: The studio covers a variety of topics including the release of a new biography on Carl Rove, updates on international relations with Iran, news regarding Supreme Court Justice Anthony M. Kennedy's health, and reports on professional athletes like Adeball Sanchez and Latrell Spreewell.
